top_signals.title: China Could Be About to Remove Oil's Biggest Safety Net
top_signals.summary: The oil market may soon run out of the supply and demand cushions that have kept prices from soaring to record highs during the huge loss of flows through the Strait of Hormuz. The window provided by the U.S.-Iran memorandum of understanding, during which Middle Eastern producers rushed the crude amassed in the Gulf in the previous four months out of the region, abruptly shut down with the renewed hostilities and all-but-dead ceasefire. Inventories of crude and fuels in key markets, including the United States, are running dangerously low with…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Jefferson, Navigating Economic Shocks: A Monetary Policymaker’s Perspective
top_signals.summary: Speech At the Stanford Institute for Economic Policy Research, Stanford University, Stanford, California

top_signals.title: Heartstopper Forever review: Bucketloads more queer joy from Netflix
top_signals.summary: Heartstopper Forever review and star rating: ★★★★ “Everyone thinks teenage relationships don’t last,” teases Joe Locke’s Charlie Spring in the feature length finale of Heartstopper. To be fair, they tend not to. But it’s not like these characters have ever really been constrained by reality. In Netflix’s LGBTQ teen romance series, adapted from Alice Oseman’s [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.
